1	# HarborGate — System Design
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	HarborGate is a browser-based Visitor Access & Content Compliance system. It runs entirely client-side with no backend server. All state is managed in the browser using IndexedDB and LocalStorage, with Web Crypto API for encryption at rest.
6	
7	## Architecture
8	
9	### Single-Page Application (SPA)
10	
11	- Entry point: `frontend/index.html`
12	- Hash-based routing (`#/dashboard`, `#/reservations`, `#/map`, etc.)
13	- No build tools, no frameworks — vanilla HTML/CSS/JavaScript
14	
15	### Layer Structure
16	
17	```
18	┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
19	│              Views (UI Layer)           │
20	│  dashboard, reservations, map, unlock,  │
21	│  notifications, content, admin, settings│
22	├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
23	│           Components (Shared UI)        │
24	│  Modal, Drawer, Table, Notifications,   │
25	│  SessionWarning                         │
26	├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
27	│           Services (Business Logic)     │
28	│  auth, permissions, device, map, cms,   │
29	│  notifications, audit, importexport     │
30	├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
31	│         Core Modules                    │
32	│  store.js, router.js, database.js,      │
33	│  crypto.js                              │
34	├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
35	│         Browser Storage                 │
36	│  IndexedDB (primary) + LocalStorage     │
37	│  (settings/session)                     │
38	└─────────────────────────────────────────┘
39	```
40	
41	## Core Modules
42	
43	### store.js
44	Lightweight reactive state store. Coordinates Views, Modals, Drawers, and Table pagination/sorting. Provides `get(key)`, `set(key, value)`, `getAll()`, and `subscribe(listener)`.
45	
46	### router.js
47	Hash-based router. Maps URL fragments to view render functions. Provides `register(hash, renderFn)`, `navigate(hash)`, and `currentRoute()`.
48	
49	### database.js
50	IndexedDB wrapper with encryption at rest. Provides async CRUD (`get`, `getAll`, `getByIndex`, `getOneByIndex`, `add`, `put`, `remove`, `clear`, `count`). The `audit_logs` store is append-only — `put`, `remove`, and `clear` are rejected for it.
51	
52	### crypto.js
53	Web Crypto API helpers. PBKDF2 key derivation for password hashing and key encryption. AES-GCM encryption/decryption for data at rest (via shared DEK wrapped per-user with password-derived KEK) and import/export bundles.
54	
55	## Data Model (IndexedDB Stores)
56	
57	| Store | Key | Description |
58	|-------|-----|-------------|
59	| users | id | User accounts with hashed passwords, roles, lockout state |
60	| roles | id | Role definitions and permission sets |
61	| reservations | id | Visit reservations with time windows |
62	| entry_permissions | id | Time-bound entry permissions linked to reservations |
63	| devices | id | Door/device registry |
64	| command_outbox | id | Queued device commands for offline fault tolerance |
65	| pois | id | Points of interest with feet-based coordinates |
66	| content | id | CMS content items with version history |
67	| reports | id | Compliance reports with evidence chains |
68	| audit_logs | id | Immutable audit trail entries |
69	| notifications | id | Notification inbox items |
70	| rate_limits | id | Admin-configurable rate-limit rules |
71	| zones | id | Zone definitions |
72	| geofences | id | Polygon geofence boundaries |
73	
74	## Authentication & Sessions
75	
76	- Local-only username/password authentication
77	- Password policy: min 12 chars, 1 upper, 1 lower, 1 number, 1 symbol
78	- PBKDF2 key derivation (100,000 iterations, SHA-256) for password hashing and KEK derivation
79	- Shared Data Encryption Key (DEK) wrapped per-user with password-derived KEK for cross-role data access
80	- 5 failed attempts → 15-minute account lockout
81	- Session token stored in LocalStorage
82	- 30-minute idle timeout; "Extend Session" prompt at 25 minutes
83	- All sensitive fields (password hashes, salts, device secrets) never rendered in UI
84	
85	## Roles & Permissions
86	
87	| Role | Access |
88	|------|--------|
89	| Visitor | Dashboard, Reservations (own), Map, Notifications |
90	| Operator | + Remote Unlock, all Reservations |
91	| Reviewer | + Content moderation, CMS review queue |
92	| Administrator | Full access, User management, Audit logs, Reports |
93	
94	## Entry Permissions
95	
96	- Auto-generated when a reservation is approved
97	- Time window: 15 min before → 30 min after reservation start
98	- Single-use: consumed on first successful unlock
99	- Multi-use: up to 5 entries allowed
100	- Status badge: Active / Used / Expired / Pending
101	
102	## Device Service Layer
103	
104	- Simulated device integration via frontend Service layer
105	- Supports HTTP, MQTT, WebSocket adapters (local network only)
106	- ACK required within 2 seconds → else command enters queued state
107	- Retry every 10 seconds for up to 2 minutes
108	- Local command outbox persists queued commands in IndexedDB
109	
110	## Venue Map
111	
112	- SVG-based map with POI markers
113	- Coordinates in feet-based display
114	- Search modes: radius, administrative zone, polygon geofence
115	- Canvas overlay for drawing polygon geofences
116	- Route planning: suggested entry points + estimated walk time
117	- Configurable walk speed (default 3 mph), no external map providers
118	
119	## Notification System
120	
121	- In-app inbox for approvals, overdue items, missing materials
122	- Template engine with variable substitution: `{reservationId}`, `{doorName}`, etc.
123	- Scheduled reminders: 24h and 1h before reservation start
124	- Local retry: up to 3 attempts per notification
125	- Delivered/failed receipts shown in UI
126	
127	## CMS Workflow
128	
129	```
130	Draft → Review → Published → Archived
131	```
132	- Multilingual variants per content item
133	- Version history with diff view
134	- Rollback to any previous version
135	
136	## Audit Trail
137	
138	- Immutable entries written for all privileged actions
139	- Timestamp format: MM/DD/YYYY, 12-hour time (e.g., 04/02/2026, 02:30:00 PM)
140	- Fields: timestamp, actor ID, actor role, action, before snapshot, after snapshot
141	
142	## Import/Export
143	
144	- Browser-side Blob downloads and file pickers
145	- Mandatory AES-GCM encrypted JSON bundles (password required for both export and import)
146	- Plaintext export/import is not permitted
147	- Covers all IndexedDB stores for full backup/migration
148	
149	## Deployment
150	
151	- Docker: nginx:alpine serving `frontend/` as static files on port 8080
152	- No server-side processing required
153

### 2. database.js (Lines 1-30 and Full File)

**Key Features:**
- IndexedDB wrapper for HarborGate data
- Supports encryption at rest via session-derived CryptoKey
- DB_NAME = 'harborgate', DB_VERSION = 2
- **ENCRYPTED_STORES** set includes: reservations, entry_permissions, devices, pois, content, reports, audit_logs, notifications, command_outbox, rate_limits, zones, geofences
- **Intentionally UNENCRYPTED**: users, roles (must be readable pre-auth for login/password verification)

**Stores Defined (14 total):**

**Key Methods:**
- `setEncryptionKey()`, `clearEncryptionKey()`, `getEncryptionKey()`
- `encryptIfNeeded()`, `decryptIfNeeded()`, `decryptArray()`
- CRUD: `getAll()`, `get()`, `getByIndex()`, `getOneByIndex()`, `add()`, `put()`, `remove()`, `clear()`, `count()`

### 3. crypto.js (Lines 100-160 and Full File)

**Lines 100-160 (At-Rest Encryption Methods):**
```javascript

```

**notifications.js service (113 lines):**

- `deliverNotification(notification)` - Deliver or mark failed
- `retryFailedNotifications()` - Retry pending with retryCount < MAX_RETRIES
- `processScheduledNotifications()` - Process due scheduled notifications
- `scheduleReservationReminders(reservation)` - Schedule 24h and 1h reminders
- `getTemplates()` - Get all templates
- `getUserNotifications(userId)` - Get user's notifications

- Delete notifications
- Search and filter
- Real-time delivery status
- Template variable preview

### Summary of Key Architectural Decisions

1. **No Backend** - Entirely client-side with IndexedDB persistence
2. **Encryption at Rest** - AES-GCM with PBKDF2 key derivation, DEK/KEK separation
3. **Pure Business Logic** - `/lib/` modules with no browser dependencies for testability

6. **Audit Trail** - Immutable append-only logs for all privileged actions
7. **Rate Limiting** - Per-user and global login attempt limits
8. **Session Management** - localStorage storage, 30-min idle timeout, 25-min warning prompt
9. **Local Retry** - Notifications attempt up to 3 times before failing
10. **Time Windows** - Entry permissions with flexible 15-min before / 30-min after windows

1	# HarborGate — System Design
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	HarborGate is a browser-based Visitor Access & Content Compliance system. It runs entirely client-side with no backend server. All state is managed in the browser using IndexedDB and LocalStorage, with Web Crypto API for encryption at rest.
6	
7	## Architecture
8	
9	### Single-Page Application (SPA)
10	
11	- Entry point: `frontend/index.html`
12	- Hash-based routing (`#/dashboard`, `#/reservations`, `#/map`, etc.)
13	- No build tools, no frameworks — vanilla HTML/CSS/JavaScript
14	
15	### Layer Structure
16	
17	```
18	┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
19	│              Views (UI Layer)           │
20	│  dashboard, reservations, map, unlock,  │
21	│  notifications, content, admin, settings│
22	├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
23	│           Components (Shared UI)        │
24	│  Modal, Drawer, Table, Notifications,   │
25	│  SessionWarning                         │

40	
41	## Core Modules
42	
43	### store.js
44	Lightweight reactive state store. Coordinates Views, Modals, Drawers, and Table pagination/sorting. Provides `get(key)`, `set(key, value)`, `getAll()`, and `subscribe(listener)`.
45	
46	### router.js
47	Hash-based router. Maps URL fragments to view render functions. Provides `register(hash, renderFn)`, `navigate(hash)`, and `currentRoute()`.
48	
49	### database.js
50	IndexedDB wrapper with encryption at rest. Provides async CRUD (`get`, `getAll`, `getByIndex`, `getOneByIndex`, `add`, `put`, `remove`, `clear`, `count`). The `audit_logs` store is append-only — `put`, `remove`, and `clear` are rejected for it.
51	
52	### crypto.js
53	Web Crypto API helpers. PBKDF2 key derivation for password hashing and key encryption. AES-GCM encryption/decryption for data at rest (via shared DEK wrapped per-user with password-derived KEK) and import/export bundles.
54	
55	## Data Model (IndexedDB Stores)
56	
57	| Store | Key | Description |
58	|-------|-----|-------------|
59	| users | id | User accounts with hashed passwords, roles, lockout state |
60	| roles | id | Role definitions and permission sets |
61	| reservations | id | Visit reservations with time windows |
62	| entry_permissions | id | Time-bound entry permissions linked to reservations |
63	| devices | id | Door/device registry |
64	| command_outbox | id | Queued device commands for offline fault tolerance |
65	| pois | id | Points of interest with feet-based coordinates |
66	| content | id | CMS content items with version history |
67	| reports | id | Compliance reports with evidence chains |
68	| audit_logs | id | Immutable audit trail entries |
69	| notifications | id | Notification inbox items |
70	| rate_limits | id | Admin-configurable rate-limit rules |
71	| zones | id | Zone definitions |
72	| geofences | id | Polygon geofence boundaries |
73	
74	## Authentication & Sessions
75	
76	- Local-only username/password authentication
77	- Password policy: min 12 chars, 1 upper, 1 lower, 1 number, 1 symbol
78	- PBKDF2 key derivation (100,000 iterations, SHA-256) for password hashing and KEK derivation
79	- Shared Data Encryption Key (DEK) wrapped per-user with password-derived KEK for cross-role data access
80	- 5 failed attempts → 15-minute account lockout
81	- Session token stored in LocalStorage
82	- 30-minute idle timeout; "Extend Session" prompt at 25 minutes
83	- All sensitive fields (password hashes, salts, device secrets) never rendered in UI
84	
85	## Roles & Permissions
86	
87	| Role | Access |
88	|------|--------|
89	| Visitor | Dashboard, Reservations (own), Map, Notifications |
90	| Operator | + Remote Unlock, all Reservations |
91	| Reviewer | + Content moderation, CMS review queue |
92	| Administrator | Full access, User management, Audit logs, Reports |
93	
94	## Entry Permissions
95	
96	- Auto-generated when a reservation is approved
97	- Time window: 15 min before → 30 min after reservation start
98	- Single-use: consumed on first successful unlock
99	- Multi-use: up to 5 entries allowed
100	- Status badge: Active / Used / Expired / Pending
101	
102	## Device Service Layer
103	
104	- Simulated device integration via frontend Service layer
105	- Supports HTTP, MQTT, WebSocket adapters (local network only)
106	- ACK required within 2 seconds → else command enters queued state
107	- Retry every 10 seconds for up to 2 minutes
108	- Local command outbox persists queued commands in IndexedDB
109	
110	## Venue Map
111	
112	- SVG-based map with POI markers
113	- Coordinates in feet-based display
114	- Search modes: radius, administrative zone, polygon geofence
115	- Canvas overlay for drawing polygon geofences
116	- Route planning: suggested entry points + estimated walk time
117	- Configurable walk speed (default 3 mph), no external map providers
118	
119	## Notification System
120	
121	- In-app inbox for approvals, overdue items, missing materials
122	- Template engine with variable substitution: `{reservationId}`, `{doorName}`, etc.
123	- Scheduled reminders: 24h and 1h before reservation start
124	- Local retry: up to 3 attempts per notification
125	- Delivered/failed receipts shown in UI
126	
127	## CMS Workflow
128	
129	```
130	Draft → Review → Published → Archived
131	```
132	- Multilingual variants per content item
133	- Version history with diff view
134	- Rollback to any previous version
135	
136	## Audit Trail
137	
138	- Immutable entries written for all privileged actions
139	- Timestamp format: MM/DD/YYYY, 12-hour time (e.g., 04/02/2026, 02:30:00 PM)
140	- Fields: timestamp, actor ID, actor role, action, before snapshot, after snapshot
141	
142	## Import/Export
143	
144	- Browser-side Blob downloads and file pickers
145	- Mandatory AES-GCM encrypted JSON bundles (password required for both export and import)
146	- Plaintext export/import is not permitted
147	- Covers all IndexedDB stores for full backup/migration
148	
149	## Deployment
150	
151	- Docker: nginx:alpine serving `frontend/` as static files on port 8080
152	- No server-side processing required
